column1: Table 1
Question Suggestions
Please educate me on a recent event you organized for a company as if I am entirely new to the space.

This role requires a great degree of autonomy and a bias towards execution. Tell me about a time you identified and executed an initiative outside of your formal scope of work and describe the impact it had on your work and on others.
Tell me about a time that empathy guided a significant decision you had to make
What is inspiring you to seek this position?
When I talk with your references, what will they say your weaknesses are?
What motivated you to reach out to us?
What aspect of your background do you feel will be of greatest value to the company?
What is your interest/experience in technology?  how about mental health?
What did you do to prepare yourself to chat with me today?
Based on your research, how would you describe what we do?
Looking at the greatest accomplishment on your resume, what could you have improved?
What was the last thing you determined you needed to learn?
How did you go about that?
Do you prefer to work in teams or more independently?
When have you taken a leadership position?
Give me 5 bullet points on your life (personal, professional, fun).
Tell me about a time you failed. What did you learn from it?
Where do you see yourself in 5 years?
How do you spend your free time?
What's your experience with teamwork  - pros and cons?
How long have you known x and what has been the nature of the relationship?
What are his/her greatest strengths?
What can you tell me about X’s greatest achievement in the time that you have known him?
How does he/she establish priorities and make decisions?
What drives him/her?
What are his/her greatest areas of development and is he/she aware of his development areas?
How have you seen x cope and manage through adverse situations?
How does he/she communicate?
Can you describe how he/she behaves when an idea is introduced or a decision made that he/she doesn’t agree with?
How does he/she receive feedback?

It is important that we bring people into our organization that we deem brave. Can you talk about a time when he/she acted unconventionally or bravely? This could be a challenging decision or an innovative idea he/she drove.
Would you work with him/her again?
Is there anything else I should ask you that I have not yet asked?
